purpose: A minimal API gateway that routes incoming HTTP requests to upstream module endpoints based on a configurable route table, aggregates responses, and handles basic error translation. Provides a unified entry point for composed module systems.

endpoints.description: Proxy request to configured upstream module

sidecar_specification.purpose: Route aggregator and error translator for composed modules

agent_usage.when_to_use: When multiple modules need a single HTTP entry point with unified error handling
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: When client can call individual modules directly or when load balancing is required

failure_modes.recovery: Check upstream module health; retry once

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not call external URLs not in route table
Must not cache responses without explicit Cache-Control

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use native http.request or node-fetch. Load route table from a local routes.json file at startup. Support wildcard path segments.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Response body streams must be consumed or piped. Do NOT use Express res.json() on proxy responses unless you buffer the body.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Create a RouteTable class that maps path patterns to { upstreamHost, upstreamPath }. Handle 4xx/5xx by wrapping upstream responses in a standard envelope { upstream_status, response, error }.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do NOT implement authentication, rate limiting, or request rewriting here — those are separate modules.
